event_blocks.summary: The CCTV 11 '空中剧院' broadcast from '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' features a Peking Opera performance presented in a split-screen format. The left frame showcases a Central Performer, a male actor in a purple and green robe, black beard, and traditional headpiece, standing atop a stone gate prop. He delivers a dramatic, high-pitched operatic monologue, singing lyrics displayed as subtitles such as '我本是卧龙岗散淡的人', '凭阴阳如反掌保定乾坤', '先帝爷下南阳御驾三请', '算就了汉家的业鼎足三分', '官封到武乡侯执掌帅印', '东西战南北剿博古通今', and '周文王访姜尚周室大振'. The performer gestures expressively with a white feather fan, opens and closes it, and occasionally adjusts his beard, his facial expressions intense. The right frame displays Orchestra Members in formal black attire, seated behind a wooden railing against a red patterned backdrop. They play traditional Chinese instruments including the erhu, pipa, and drum, providing a synchronized, intricate, and rich musical accompaniment that blends seamlessly with the performer's vocal delivery. The stage set includes a blue curtain and red drapes, resembling a historical city gate, enhancing the theatrical ambiance under consistent lighting. The performance culminates with the performer's vocal flourish, the orchestra's swell, and the sound of enthusiastic audience applause.

Subtitles.description: On-screen Chinese text displaying the lyrics of the performance, including '官封到武乡侯执掌帅印', '东西战南北剿博古通今', and '周文王访姜尚周室大振', which are being sung by the Central Performer, indicating the musical style and dialogue.

Orchestra Members.description: A group of musicians in black formal attire playing traditional Chinese instruments including pipa, erhu, and drum, seated behind a wooden railing with a red patterned backdrop, visible in a smaller inset on the right side of the split-screen, providing live, intricate musical accompaniment to the performance.

CCTV 11 Logo and Text.description: On-screen graphics including the CCTV 11 logo, '空中剧院' (Air Theatre), and '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' (Beijing Meilanfang Grand Theatre), identifying the broadcast and venue.

Central Performer.description: A male actor in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, wearing a black beard and a traditional headpiece, holding a white feather fan, delivering a dramatic monologue atop a stone gate prop, singing in a high-pitched, stylized Peking Opera vocal style, occasionally gesturing with the fan and adjusting his beard.

Stage Set.description: A theatrical stage with a blue curtain, a gray stone gate prop, and red drapes, designed to resemble a historical city gate, providing a dramatic backdrop for the performance.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide shot of a Peking Opera stage, featuring a city gate set with a blue curtain and a gray stone prop. Performers in elaborate traditional costumes are visible, including a central male actor in a purple and green robe with a black beard and feathered fan, positioned atop the gate. A lively and intricate traditional Chinese orchestral arrangement immediately begins, characterized by prominent string instruments like the erhu and pipa, along with a distinct percussive beat. The scene then cuts to a close-up of the orchestra, where musicians in formal black attire are seen playing these instruments behind a wooden railing against a red patterned backdrop, guided by a conductor. Returning to the stage, the central performer delivers a dramatic, high-pitched operatic monologue in a stylized Peking Opera vocal style, his powerful voice resonating with the synchronized orchestral accompaniment. Subtitles appear, displaying '[西皮慢三眼]' and the line '我本是卧龙岗散淡的人' (I was originally a carefree man from Wolong Hill), which the performer sings with expressive, melismatic phrases. The camera alternates between the performer and the orchestra, emphasizing their seamless integration. This theatrical and traditional presentation, brightly lit and formally broadcast as CCTV 11's '空中剧院' from the '北京·梅兰芳大剧院', culminates with the performer's vocal flourish and the orchestra's swell, followed by the distinct sound of enthusiastic audience applause.

The video opens with a wide shot of Orchestra Members, seated behind a wooden railing against a red patterned backdrop, playing traditional Chinese instruments like the pipa, erhu, and drum, creating a rich, traditional musical accompaniment. The scene then transitions to a split-screen, with the Central Performer in a larger left frame and the Orchestra Members in a smaller inset on the right. The Central Performer, dressed in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, a black beard, and a traditional headpiece, delivers a dramatic monologue in a powerful, operatic vocal style atop a stone gate prop. He gestures subtly with a white feather fan and occasionally adjusts his beard, conveying wisdom and authority. His performance includes specific Chinese lyrics displayed as subtitles, such as '凭阴阳如反掌保定乾坤' and '先帝爷下南阳御驾三请', which are clearly heard as part of his vocal delivery, rooted in historical or legendary themes. The stage set, featuring a blue curtain and red drapes resembling a historical city gate, enhances the theatrical ambiance. The CCTV 11 logo and text, identifying '空中剧院' at '北京·梅兰芳大剧院', remain visible, while consistent lighting focuses on the solemn and culturally rich performance.

The video presents a split-screen broadcast from CCTV 11's '空中剧院' at the Beijing Meilanfang Grand Theatre, showcasing a vibrant Peking Opera performance. On the left, a male Central Performer, adorned in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, a black beard, and a traditional headpiece, delivers a powerful and highly stylized dramatic monologue atop a stone gate prop. His voice is strong and characteristic of Peking Opera, as he sings/recites the lyrics, which are simultaneously displayed as on-screen subtitles, including phrases like '先帝爷下南阳御驾三请' and '算就了汉家的业鼎足三分'. He expressively gestures with a white feather fan, opening and closing it, and occasionally adjusts his beard, his facial expressions intense and focused, conveying the gravity of the narrative. On the right, Orchestra Members, dressed in black formal attire, provide a live, synchronized musical accompaniment. Their traditional Chinese instruments, including the distinct bowing of the erhu, rhythmic drumming, and other string instruments, create a rich, melodic, and percussive soundscape that perfectly matches the performer's vocal delivery and the theatrical atmosphere. The stage set features a blue curtain and red drapes, enhancing the historical ambiance, while the CCTV 11 logo and broadcast text remain consistently displayed, reinforcing the identity of this culturally rich performance.

The video segment presents a split-screen view of a traditional Peking Opera performance, identified by the persistent CCTV 11 logo and '空中剧院' from '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' in the top corners. On the left, a Central Performer, a male actor in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, a black beard, and a traditional headpiece, stands atop a stone gate prop. He delivers a dramatic monologue, singing in a high-pitched, stylized Peking Opera vocal style, while expressively gesturing with a white feather fan and occasionally adjusting his beard. The stage behind him features a blue curtain and red drapes, creating a historical city gate ambiance, with consistent lighting highlighting the theatrical atmosphere. On the right, Orchestra Members, dressed in black formal attire, are seated behind a wooden railing with a red patterned backdrop, playing traditional Chinese instruments like the pipa, erhu, and drum, providing live, intricate musical accompaniment that blends seamlessly with the performer's singing. On-screen subtitles, such as '官封到武乡侯执掌帅印', '东西战南北剿博古通今', and '周文王访姜尚周室大振', change throughout, displaying the lyrics being sung by the performer, reflecting the historical and strategic narrative of the piece.

event_blocks.summary: The video begins with a split-screen: a Central Performer, clad in a purple and green patterned robe, black beard, and traditional headpiece, delivers an operatic monologue atop a gray stone gate prop, gesturing with a white feather fan and singing lines such as '周文王访姜尚周室大振'. The inset displays Orchestra Members in black formal attire playing traditional Chinese instruments like pipa and erhu behind a wooden railing with a red patterned backdrop. The Stage Set includes a blue curtain and the stone gate, with the CCTV 11 logo and venue text visible. At 0:13, the view transitions to a full-stage shot revealing additional performers in elaborate costumes, some in golden armor, around the gate. The Central Performer continues, bowing his head and placing the fan down while singing '闲无事在敌楼我亮一亮琴音'. At 0:44, the screen splits again, introducing a New Performer (Golden Robe) on the left, a male actor in a golden and white robe, white face, red markings, and a crown, holding a long white beard and gesturing. The Central Performer occasionally glances at the New Performer, maintaining dramatic tension under consistent spotlighting against the red and blue backdrop.

Subtitles.description: On-screen Chinese text displaying the lyrics of the performance, including '周文王访姜尚周室大振', '汉诸葛怎比得前辈的先生', and '闲无事在敌楼我亮一亮琴音', which are sung by the Central Performer.

Orchestra Members.description: A group of musicians in black formal attire skillfully playing traditional Chinese instruments including pipa and erhu, their music providing a rich, accompanying score, seated behind a wooden railing with a red patterned backdrop, visible in a smaller inset on the right side of the split-screen.

CCTV 11 Logo and Text.description: On-screen graphics including the CCTV 11 logo, '空中剧院' (Air Theatre), and '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' (Beijing Meilanfang Grand Theatre), identifying the broadcast and venue.

Stage Set.description: A theatrical stage with a blue curtain, a gray stone gate prop, and red drapes, designed to resemble a historical city gate, providing a dramatic backdrop for the performance.

Central Performer.description: A male actor in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, wearing a black beard and a traditional headpiece, holding a white feather fan, delivering a powerful, operatic monologue atop a stone gate prop, occasionally gesturing with the fan and adjusting his beard, singing lyrics such as '周文王访姜尚周室大振', '汉诸葛怎比得前辈的先生', and '闲无事在敌楼我亮一亮琴音'.

New Performer (Golden Robe).description: A male actor in a golden and white robe with a white face, red facial markings, and a crown adorned with an orange ball, holding a long white beard, appearing in a split-screen view, gesturing with his hand as if responding to the Central Performer, likely portraying a high-ranking official or divine figure.

raw_segments: The video opens with a split-screen view, featuring a Central Performer, a male actor in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, a black beard, and a traditional headpiece, delivering a powerful, operatic monologue atop a gray stone gate prop. He gestures expressively with a white feather fan and occasionally adjusts his beard, conveying a composed yet intense demeanor as he sings lyrics like '周文王访姜尚周室大振' (King Wen of Zhou visited Jiang Shang, and the Zhou Dynasty greatly prospered). The smaller inset on the right shows Orchestra Members, dressed in black formal attire, skillfully playing traditional Chinese instruments such as pipa and erhu, their music providing a rich, accompanying score behind a wooden railing with a red patterned backdrop. The Stage Set features a blue curtain and the gray stone gate prop, evoking a historical city gate, with the CCTV 11 logo and text '空中剧院' and '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' visible on-screen. As the Central Performer continues his dramatic vocal delivery, singing '汉诸葛怎比得前辈的先生' (How can Zhuge Liang of Han compare to the previous generation's master?), the view shifts at 0:13 to a full-stage shot, revealing the entire Stage Set with additional performers in elaborate costumes, some in golden armor, positioned around the gate, suggesting a military or courtly scene. The Central Performer continues his monologue with more deliberate movements, bowing his head and placing the fan down, his voice carrying the narrative, including the line '闲无事在敌楼我亮一亮琴音' (Having nothing to do, I play a tune on the enemy tower). At 0:44, the screen splits again, introducing a New Performer (Golden Robe) on the left: a male actor in a golden and white robe with a white face, red markings, and a crown adorned with an orange ball, holding a long white beard. This new figure gestures with his hand, possibly in response to the Central Performer’s ongoing sung words, creating a dynamic visual dialogue. The Central Performer remains focused, occasionally glancing toward the new figure, maintaining the dramatic tension, all while the consistent lighting spotlights the performers against the red and blue backdrop, enhancing the theatrical atmosphere.

event_blocks.summary: The performance opens with a split-screen showcasing the New Performer (Golden Robe) in a golden and white dragon-patterned robe, white face paint with red markings, and an ornate crown, holding a long white beard, and the Central Performer in a purple and green robe with a black beard, both in traditional Peking Opera attire. The stage is set with a blue curtain and red drapes, resembling a historical city gate, with the CCTV 11 logo and text visible. The New Performer (Golden Robe) is then shown in a full shot, performing dramatic gestures with his hand and a red-tasseled staff, accompanied by a powerful, stylized male operatic voice and traditional Chinese instrumental music. Subtitles display lyrics such as '我面前缺少个知音的人' and '诸葛亮在城楼饮酒抚琴', aligning with his vocal delivery and movements, emphasizing a regal or divine status. A close-up of the New Performer (Golden Robe) reveals his intricate costume and expressive face as he sings lyrics like '左右琴童人两个', accompanied by prominent strings and percussion. The camera alternates between close-ups and wider shots of the Stage Set, a gray stone gate backdrop. Subtitles then read '打扫街道俱都是老弱残兵' as the New Performer moves across the stage, interacting with a kneeling soldier in blue and red attire. A wide shot reveals the full Stage Set with the New Performer center stage, flanked by Warrior Performers in elaborate costumes. The performance concludes with the New Performer raising his staff as subtitles display '我本当領人馬殺進城', delivered with commanding operatic vocalization, reinforcing themes of military strategy.

Subtitles.description: On-screen Chinese text displaying the lyrics of the performance, including '左右琴童人两个', '打扫街道俱都是老弱残兵', and '我本当领人马杀进城', indicating the narrative and dialogue.

CCTV 11 Logo and Text.description: On-screen graphics including the CCTV 11 logo, '空中剧院' (Air Theatre), and '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' (Beijing Meilanfang Grand Theatre), identifying the broadcast and venue.

New Performer (Golden Robe).description: A male actor in a golden and white robe with intricate dragon patterns, white face with red markings, and a crown with an orange ball, holding a long white beard and red-tasseled staff, performing expressive gestures and delivering dramatic lines in a high-pitched, stylized male opera singing voice, accompanied by traditional Chinese opera music.

Central Performer.description: A male actor in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, wearing a black beard and a traditional headpiece, holding a white feather fan, delivering a monologue atop a stone gate prop, occasionally gesturing with the fan and adjusting his beard.

Stage Set.description: A theatrical stage with a blue curtain, gray stone gate prop, and red drapes, designed to resemble a historical city gate, providing a dramatic backdrop for the performance.

Kneeling Soldier.description: A male performer in a blue and red costume, kneeling on the stage, appearing as a subordinate or captive, interacting briefly with the New Performer (Golden Robe).

Warrior Performers.description: A group of performers in elaborate warrior costumes with spears and banners, positioned on the left side of the stage, adding to the military atmosphere of the scene.

raw_segments: The video opens with a split-screen, showcasing two performers in traditional Peking Opera attire. On the left, the New Performer (Golden Robe), adorned in a golden and white dragon-patterned robe, white face paint with red markings, and an ornate crown, holds a long white beard. On the right, the Central Performer, in a purple and green robe with a black beard and traditional headpiece, delivers a monologue atop a stone gate prop, occasionally gesturing with a white feather fan. The stage is set with a blue curtain and red drapes, evoking a historical city gate, while the CCTV 11 logo and '空中剧院' (Air Theatre) at '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' are visible. As the segment progresses, the view transitions to a full shot of the New Performer (Golden Robe), who performs dramatic, expressive gestures with his hand and a red-tasseled staff. Throughout this, a powerful and stylized male operatic voice, characteristic of Peking Opera, sings the narrative, accompanied by traditional Chinese instrumental music. Subtitles on screen display the sung lyrics, including '我面前缺少个知音的人' and '诸葛亮在城楼饮酒抚琴', directly aligning with the performer's vocal delivery and theatrical movements, emphasizing his regal or divine status.

The segment opens with a close-up of the New Performer (Golden Robe), a male actor in a golden and white robe adorned with intricate dragon patterns, a white face with red markings, and a crown topped with an orange ball. He holds a long white beard and a red-tasseled staff, delivering dramatic lines with expressive gestures in a high-pitched, stylized male opera singing voice, accompanied by traditional Chinese opera music featuring prominent strings and percussion. As he sings, subtitles appear on screen, displaying lyrics such as '左右琴童人两个' (On either side, two young attendants), indicating a narrative of strategic deception. The camera alternates between close-ups of his intense facial expressions and wider shots revealing the Stage Set—a theatrical backdrop of a gray stone gate with a blue curtain and red drapes. He continues his powerful operatic vocalization, with subtitles now reading '打扫街道俱都是老弱残兵' (Sweeping the streets are all old, weak, and disabled soldiers), suggesting military observation. The New Performer moves across the stage, interacting with a kneeling soldier in blue and red attire, who appears to be a subordinate or captive. The camera then pulls back to a wide shot showing the full Stage Set, where the New Performer stands center stage, flanked by other performers in elaborate warrior costumes with spears and banners. The atmosphere is tense and theatrical, emphasizing the dramatic weight of his monologue. The CCTV 11 Logo and Text, identifying the broadcast and venue as '空中剧院' (Air Theatre) at '北京·梅兰芳大剧院', remain visible throughout. The performance culminates in a powerful gesture as the New Performer raises his staff, signaling a moment of command or revelation, while the subtitles reinforce the theme of military strategy and bold decision-making with '我本当領人馬殺進城' (I should have led my troops to kill and enter the city), delivered with the same commanding operatic vocalization.

event_blocks.summary: The performance opens with a New Performer in a golden and white dragon-patterned robe, white face paint with red markings, and a crown, holding a long white beard and red-tasseled staff, delivering intense, high-pitched, operatic singing with strong vibrato, articulating lines such as '杀不得' as subtitles appear, accompanied by dramatic traditional Chinese orchestral music. The camera pulls back to reveal a stage set resembling a historical city gate with a blue curtain and red drapes, where the New Performer continues his powerful vocalization, singing lines like '又恐怕中了巧计行' and '棋逢对手一般平', flanked by Warrior Performers with spears and banners and a Kneeling Soldier. The focus shifts to the Central Performer atop the stone gate prop, dressed in a purple and green robe with a black beard and holding a white feather fan, who begins a deeper, resonant vocalization and melodic singing, with his line '我正在城楼观山景' displayed as a subtitle. Audience members are briefly shown clapping, while CCTV 11 broadcast graphics confirm the venue and program. The scene transitions to a split-screen, with the Central Performer continuing his powerful performance in the larger frame and Orchestra Members playing traditional instruments in an inset. The Central Performer's singing continues with subtitles detailing military strategy and betrayal, such as '却原来是司马发来的兵' and '马谡无谋少才能'. Later, the Central Performer sings '来来来请上城来' and '听我抚琴', gesturing with his fan, while the New Performer (Golden Robe) sings '既到此就该把城进' and '为什么犹豫不定', creating a tense dialogue. The performance maintains a dramatic and historical atmosphere with consistent lighting, focused stage elements, and continuous orchestral backing throughout.

New Performer (Golden Robe).description: A male actor in a golden and white robe with intricate dragon patterns, white face with red markings, and a crown with an orange ball, holding a long white beard and red-tasseled staff, performing expressive gestures and delivering dramatic lines with a sustained, operatic vocalization.

Warrior Performers.description: A group of performers in elaborate warrior costumes with spears and banners, positioned on the left side of the stage, adding to the military atmosphere of the scene.

Orchestra Members.description: A group of musicians in black formal attire playing traditional Chinese instruments including pipa, erhu, and drum, providing a rich instrumental accompaniment to the Central Performer's singing, visible in a smaller inset on the right side of the split-screen.

Audience Members.description: A group of men in black formal attire seated in red theater seats, clapping audibly in appreciation of the performance, visible in a brief cutaway shot.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a close-up of the New Performer (Golden Robe), a male actor in a golden and white dragon-patterned robe, white face paint with red markings, and a crown, holding a long white beard and red-tasseled staff. He performs expressive gestures while delivering intense, high-pitched, and powerful operatic singing, with strong vibrato, articulating lines such as '杀不得' as subtitles appear on screen, accompanied by dramatic traditional Chinese orchestral music featuring prominent percussion and strings. As the camera pulls back to reveal the full theatrical stage, resembling a historical city gate with a blue curtain and red drapes, the New Performer continues his powerful vocal performance, now singing lines such as '又恐怕中了巧计行' and '棋逢对手一般平'. He stands center stage, flanked by Warrior Performers in elaborate costumes with spears and banners on the left, and a Kneeling Soldier in blue and red attire on the right. The focus then shifts to the Central Performer, atop the stone gate prop, dressed in a purple and green robe with a black beard and holding a white feather fan. He begins a deeper, resonant, and sustained vocalization, characteristic of Peking Opera, followed by melodic singing, with his line '我正在城楼观山景' displayed as a subtitle. The audience is briefly shown clapping, acknowledging the performance, while the CCTV 11 broadcast graphics confirm the venue and program, maintaining a dramatic and historical atmosphere throughout with the continuous orchestral backing.

The video opens on a theatrical stage, designed as a historical city gate with a blue curtain backdrop and a gray stone gate prop, where a Central Performer, a male actor in a purple and green patterned robe, black beard, and traditional headpiece, stands atop the gate holding a white feather fan. He delivers a dramatic monologue in a powerful traditional Chinese opera singing voice, gesturing expressively with the fan and occasionally adjusting his beard, as the orchestra provides a rich accompaniment of traditional Chinese instruments. On-screen subtitles, matching his sung lyrics, reveal a narrative of military tension, starting with '耳听得城外乱纷纷' (I hear outside the city, chaos abounds) and '旌旗招展空翻影' (Banners wave, casting empty shadows). Two silent female performers in light blue robes stand on either side of him. The scene transitions to a split-screen, with the Central Performer continuing his powerful performance in the larger left frame, while a smaller inset on the right shows the Orchestra Members, musicians in black formal attire, playing traditional instruments like the pipa and erhu behind a wooden railing with a red backdrop. The Central Performer's singing continues, with subtitles like '却原来是司马发来的兵' (It turns out it's Sima's troops who have arrived), '马谡无谋少才能' (Ma Su lacked strategy and talent), and '诸葛亮在敌楼把驾等' (Zhuge Liang waits in the enemy tower), detailing a story of strategic contemplation, betrayal, and military maneuvers. The consistent lighting highlights the performer's solemn and intense tone, conveying deep thought and authority, while the CCTV 11 logo and venue details ('空中剧院', '北京·梅兰芳大剧院') remain visible throughout.

The segment opens with a male actor, the Central Performer, in a purple and green robe with intricate patterns, a black beard, and a traditional headpiece, standing atop a stone gate prop on a stage with a blue curtain and red drapes. He delivers a dramatic monologue in a high-pitched, expressive Chinese opera singing style, gesturing with a white feather fan and occasionally adjusting his beard, as on-screen subtitles display lines such as '诸葛亮在敌楼把驾等' and '谈哪谈谈谈心', indicating a strategic or psychological confrontation. The CCTV 11 logo and '空中剧院' and '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' are visible in the top corners. At 0:30, the screen splits, with the Central Performer remaining on the right. On the left, a New Performer (Golden Robe) appears, wearing a golden and white robe with dragon patterns, a white face with red markings, and a crown with an orange ball, holding a long white beard and red-tasseled staff. He performs expressive gestures, including bowing and raising his staff, also singing dramatic lines in a high-pitched Chinese opera style, as subtitles shift to lines like '既到此就该把城进' and '为什么犹豫不定', suggesting a tense dialogue. The Central Performer responds with gestures and lines such as '来来来请上城来', continuing the dramatic vocal exchange. The stage set, with its stone gate and red drapes, provides a historical, theatrical backdrop, and focused lighting enhances the dramatic atmosphere throughout the segment, all accompanied by traditional Chinese opera instrumental music.

The minute opens with a split-screen view of a theatrical stage, where the Central Performer, in a purple and green robe with a black beard and traditional headpiece, delivers a dramatic monologue atop a stone gate prop. He sings in a high-pitched, traditional Chinese operatic style, his voice expressive and commanding, as subtitles display his lines, first '来来来请上城来' (Come, come, please come up to the city) and later '听我抚琴' (Listen to me play the zither). He gestures with a white feather fan and adjusts his beard, maintaining an authoritative demeanor against the backdrop of a blue curtain and red drapes. The camera then focuses on a full-screen close-up of the Central Performer, highlighting his facial expressions and the intricate patterns of his costume. A brief cutaway shows several audience members in black formal attire clapping enthusiastically, their applause clearly audible. The scene concludes with the New Performer (Golden Robe) now center stage, adorned in a golden and white dragon-patterned robe, white face paint, and a crown, holding a long white beard and a red-tasseled staff. He delivers dramatic lines with expressive gestures, his voice continuing the sustained, operatic vocalization, as the CCTV 11 logo and '空中剧院' at '北京·梅兰芳大剧院' remain visible.
